title: Transforming Sketches into Vibrant Art with Stable Diffusion SDXL
description:
Join me in today's tutorial as we explore the magic of Stable Diffusion SDXL to turn simple sketches into colorful digital art! 

Follow along as we set up the necessary extensions and models, and witness the transformation of a basic owl sketch into a variety of art styles - digital, watercolor, fantasy, and Impressionist. With easy drag-and-drop features, and adjustable settings to suit your style, creating vibrant illustrations has never been more straightforward. 
Whether you're looking to perfect a cartoonish autumn scene or a spooky Halloween vibe, SDXL has got you covered! 

Find the resources from the video at the following links:

Juggernaut XL V4.5 Model
https://civitai.com/models/133005?modelVersionId=162509

Install sd-webui-controlnet extension on Automatic1111
More about control net here https://github.com/Mikubill/sd-webui-controlnet

Download kohya_controllllite_xl_canny.safetensors from 
https://huggingface.co/lllyasviel/sd_control_collection/tree/main
and put it in yo
transcript: Hi everyone! Today, I want to talk about how you can transform a sketch or line art into a finished,  coloured image using stable diffusion SD-XL automatic 1111. Let's get started.  Go to extensions, then to the available tab, and click the load from button.  Use the control plus F shortcut to search for SD web UI control net. When you find it,  you should see an install button, mine is already installed. After you've installed the extension,  you can restart stable diffusion, and you should see a tab labeled control net.  Now, this extension also needs some models to work its magic. There are all kinds of models  like Kenny, Depth, and OpenPose. At the time of recording this video, I didn't find a line art  model for SD-XL, but I found an alternative that works. On the Hugging Face website,  there's a user named Lyosfield who has different models. I'll provide the link in the description.  You'll need to download the Kojaya control light XL Kenny model. In the Web UI folder,  look for extensions, then SD web UI control net. Inside, there's a folder called models where you will  place all your models for control net. Now, control net is set up. The model we just downloaded only  works with SD-XL models, and if you're interested, I am using Juggernaut XL version 4.5.  Let's open the control net tab. You can drag and drop or click to upload an image there.  I've drawn a quick sketch of an owl in Adobe Photoshop and saved it at 1024px.  Make sure the enable and pixel-perfect boxes are checked. For control type,  look for line art and it should automatically add a pre-processor named line art standard  from white background and black line. On the right, where it says model, choose Kojaya control light  XL Kenny that we downloaded earlier. Click refresh if it doesn't appear. Now let's test a prompt.  I will use Acute Cartoonal, wearing a brown bow tie and a hat with leaves, fantasy autumn forest,  cozy and cute. Then on SD-XL styles, check my other video about this extension, choose digital art.  When you are ready, hit the generate button.  Depending on your video card and a few seconds, you will have transformed the sketch into a final  color digital illustration. As you can see, it has a bow tie, a hat with leaves, and is sitting on  a branch just like I did in my sketch. You can see the before and after, it looks pretty good in  my opinion. What do you think? Let's test another style, like watercolor, which is a very  popular art style these days. Now I have a different illustration from the same sketch. This one looks  good too. Let's try another one, maybe a fantasy art style to see what we get.  The result is nice and clean. I like this because it lets me decide the composition and what to  have in the illustration that way the AI is more of a tool. And for the last art style, I will try  the one called Impressionist. The result is great. Of course, you can hit generate multiple times  and get different variations of it. In the control net settings, you have different options like  control net weight. If you lower the value, it will have less influence on the result and the image  will look slightly different from the sketch. You can also decide at what steps the influence of  the control net model should start or end. There are also options like, my prompt is more important  or control net is more important. I'll try a value for control weight of 0.4 to see what we get.  And as you can see from the result, we have the owl in the hat on a branch, but the pose is  slightly different. I'll reset the weight back to one and I'll try another prompt for winter  this time. Acute cartoon white owl wearing a red bow tie and a hat, fantasy winter forest,  cozy and cute. The result looks great. I can see myself using something like this for Christmas card.  Let's try another sketch. This time I made a sketch of a Halloween pumpkin and some ghosts  in Photoshop. For the prompt, I will use two cute cartoon ghosts coming from behind a  Halloween pumpkin, mushroom, rock, dark and moody, eerie lights, grass. And I'll hit  generate to see what I can get. The result looks interesting, but I'm not sure I want the impressionist  art style for this one. I'll try another style, the fantasy art.  I like this style, the ghosts and pumpkin look great. It has that Halloween mood I was looking for.  In this generation, I got a ghost pumpkin also. Let me try the control net is more important  function to see how it affects the result. I love the result. What do you think?  Leave a comment on the video or you can join my Facebook group called Pixeloma Community.  You can post your results or questions there and you can also participate in our daily AI challenge.  Like, share and subscribe to help me grow and allow me to do more tutorials for you. Have a great day.
